chapter: Chapter 8: Force and Laws of Motion

questions.question: Explain the term force with an example.
questions.answer: Force is a push or pull acting on an object that can change its motion. Example: pushing a box.

questions.question: List any two effects of force.
questions.answer: Force can change the speed of an object and change its direction of motion.

questions.question: Differentiate between balanced and unbalanced forces.
questions.answer: Balanced forces do not change the motion of an object, while unbalanced forces cause a change in motion.

questions.question: Define inertia and give one example.
questions.answer: Inertia is the tendency of an object to resist change in its state of motion. Example: passengers move forward when a bus stops suddenly.

questions.question: What are the types of inertia?
questions.answer: Inertia of rest, inertia of motion, and inertia of direction.

questions.question: State Newton’s First Law of Motion.
questions.answer: An object remains at rest or continues to move with uniform velocity unless acted upon by an unbalanced force.

questions.question: Why is Newton’s First Law called the law of inertia?
questions.answer: Because it explains the tendency of objects to resist changes in their state of motion.

questions.question: Define momentum.
questions.answer: Momentum is the product of mass and velocity of an object.

questions.question: Write the SI unit of momentum.
questions.answer: The SI unit of momentum is kilogram metre per second (kg m/s).

questions.question: Write the formula for momentum.
questions.answer: Momentum = mass × velocity (p = mv).

questions.question: State Newton’s Second Law of Motion.
questions.answer: The rate of change of momentum of an object is proportional to the applied force.

questions.question: Write the mathematical form of Newton’s Second Law.
questions.answer: F = ma, where F is force, m is mass and a is acceleration.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of force?
questions.answer: The SI unit of force is Newton (N).

questions.question: Define one Newton.
questions.answer: One Newton is the force that produces acceleration of 1 m/s² in a mass of 1 kg.

questions.question: State Newton’s Third Law of Motion.
questions.answer: For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction.

questions.question: Give one example of Newton’s Third Law.
questions.answer: A gun recoils backward when a bullet is fired forward.

questions.question: What is friction?
questions.answer: Friction is the force that opposes motion between two surfaces in contact.

questions.question: Why do objects stop moving after some time?
questions.answer: Objects stop moving because friction opposes their motion.

questions.question: What is the relationship between mass and inertia?
questions.answer: Inertia increases with increase in mass.

questions.question: Why do passengers fall backward when a bus starts suddenly?
questions.answer: Due to inertia of rest.

questions.question: What is acceleration?
questions.answer: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity.

questions.question: Write the SI unit of acceleration.
questions.answer: The SI unit of acceleration is m/s².

questions.question: Name the three laws of motion.
questions.answer: Newton’s First Law, Second Law and Third Law of Motion.

questions.question: Which quantity measures inertia?
questions.answer: Mass.

questions.question: Who proposed the laws of motion?
questions.answer: Sir Isaac Newton.
